nodes.name: ecc-agent-data-home.mdc
nodes.summary: Cursor rules file that configures isolated ECC memory persistence for Cursor IDE, pointing session data to ~/.cursor/ecc instead of ~/.claude and explaining the ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME override.

nodes.name: build-opencode.js
nodes.summary: Build script that compiles the .opencode TypeScript distribution using the project-local TypeScript compiler (tsc) with the .opencode/tsconfig.json configuration.

nodes.name: catalog.js
nodes.summary: CI catalog sync and validation script that parses README and agent doc count expectations, evaluates them against the actual catalog of skills/commands/agents, and syncs English and Chinese documentation files to match.

nodes.name: parseReadmeExpectations
nodes.summary: Parses numeric count expectations (skills, commands, agents, rules) from the English README using regex matches for CI validation.

nodes.name: syncEnglishReadme
nodes.summary: Updates the English README by replacing catalog count placeholders with current catalog values across all tracked sections.

nodes.name: createDocumentSpecs
nodes.summary: Builds the list of document sync specifications that pair each documentation file with its parser and sync callbacks for catalog consistency checking.

nodes.name: runCatalogCheck
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full catalog check: builds catalog, syncs documents, evaluates expectations, and returns pass/fail results.

nodes.name: check-unicode-safety.js
nodes.summary: CI security script that scans all text files for dangerous Unicode characters (invisible code points, bidirectional overrides, homoglyphs) and optionally auto-sanitizes them.

nodes.name: isDangerousInvisibleCodePoint
nodes.summary: Classifies a Unicode code point as dangerous and invisible (zero-width, bidi override, invisible separator, etc.) that could be used for prompt injection attacks.

nodes.name: sanitizeText
nodes.summary: Strips dangerous invisible characters and normalizes suspicious Unicode sequences from text content for safe storage.

nodes.name: generate-command-registry.js
nodes.summary: CI script that scans all command Markdown files, extracts descriptions and references to agents/skills, infers command types, and writes or checks a JSON command registry file.

nodes.name: generateRegistry
nodes.summary: Scans command files to build the full registry object with per-command metadata, agent/skill usage counts, and top usage statistics.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry-point function that parses CLI args and executes check, write, or print-summary modes for the command registry.

nodes.name: scan-supply-chain-iocs.js
nodes.summary: CI supply-chain security scanner that walks source files and node_modules to detect indicators of compromise (IOCs): malicious package patterns, credential theft targets, and suspicious Claude settings modifications.

nodes.name: scanFile
nodes.summary: Core IOC scanner for a single file: checks against all indicator patterns, crypto theft patterns, and permission manipulation patterns, emitting findings with severity levels.

nodes.name: claudePermissionDenyRanges
nodes.summary: Parses a Claude settings JSON file and returns character ranges for permission denyList entries to enable targeted IOC detection within permission blocks.

nodes.name: supply-chain-advisory-sources.js
nodes.summary: CI utility that fetches and validates supply-chain advisory feeds (npm audit, GitHub advisories, OSSF Scorecard) to keep the IOC scanner's data sources current.

nodes.name: validateSources
nodes.summary: Validates all configured advisory sources by checking their fetch status and ensuring they are reachable, returning structured pass/fail results.

nodes.name: requestUrl
nodes.summary: HTTP/HTTPS request helper with timeout support, returning status, headers, and response body for advisory source validation.

nodes.name: validate-agents.js
nodes.summary: CI validator that reads all agent Markdown files, parses their YAML frontmatter, and checks required fields (name, description, tools, model) for compliance.

nodes.name: validate-commands.js
nodes.summary: CI validator that checks all command Markdown files for required frontmatter (description), consistent naming, and valid agent/skill cross-references.

nodes.name: validate-hooks.js
nodes.summary: CI validator that reads the hooks configuration from settings.json, checks each hook entry for valid matchers, command existence, and required fields.

nodes.name: validateHooks
nodes.summary: Main hook validation loop that iterates hook groups, validates each entry, and aggregates errors with file-reference context.

nodes.name: validate-install-manifests.js
nodes.summary: CI validator that reads all plugin install manifest JSON files, verifies their schema, checks referenced files exist, and ensures no stale paths are listed.

nodes.name: validateInstallManifests
nodes.summary: Primary validation routine for install manifests that verifies all file entries exist, checks manifest schema compliance, and reports errors.

nodes.name: validate-no-personal-paths.js
nodes.summary: CI security validator that scans all committed files for personal path leaks (e.g., /Users/username/ hard-coded paths) that should not appear in public source.

nodes.name: validate-rules.js
nodes.summary: CI validator that collects all rule Markdown files and checks each one for required content structure and naming conventions.

nodes.name: validate-skills.js
nodes.summary: CI validator that inspects skill directories for required SKILL.md presence, valid frontmatter fields, and compliance with skill placement policy.

nodes.name: validate-workflow-security.js
nodes.summary: CI security validator for GitHub Actions workflow files that detects dangerous patterns: unpinned actions, missing permission scopes, and unsafe checkout configurations.

nodes.name: findViolations
nodes.summary: Scans a parsed workflow YAML object for security violations including unpinned third-party actions, overly permissive scopes, and missing security headers.

nodes.name: claw.js
nodes.summary: NanoClaw v2 — a zero-dependency interactive REPL for Claude Code that wraps `claude -p` with persistent session management, history compaction, and session branching stored in ~/.claude/claw/.

nodes.name: askClaude
nodes.summary: Invokes the `claude -p` subprocess with model, session resume flags, and full conversation history, returning the model's text response.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Primary REPL loop for claw.js: parses CLI args, dispatches subcommands (sessions, export, branch, compact), and runs the interactive prompt loop with history persistence.

nodes.name: generate.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript codemap generator that walks the source directory, classifies files by area (frontend, backend, database, etc.), and produces structured Markdown architecture documentation under docs/CODEMAPS/.

nodes.name: generateAreaDoc
nodes.summary: Renders a structured Markdown document for a single codebase area with directory trees, key files, and architectural summaries.

nodes.name: pre-commit
nodes.summary: ECC Codex git pre-commit hook that scans staged file diffs for high-signal secrets (API keys, tokens, private key blocks) and blocks the commit if any are found.

nodes.name: pre-push
nodes.summary: ECC Codex git pre-push hook that detects the project's package manager and runs lint, typecheck, test, and build scripts (and optionally audit) before allowing a push.

nodes.name: check-codex-global-state.sh
nodes.summary: Shell diagnostic script for the Codex environment that checks global state including plugin cache integrity, git hook installation, and ECC configuration consistency.

nodes.name: check-plugin-cache.js
nodes.summary: Codex utility that validates the Claude Code plugin cache directory, listing installed versions, checking manifest references, and detecting stale or corrupt cache entries.

nodes.name: checkCache
nodes.summary: Main cache validation routine that iterates installed plugin versions, checks for manifest references, and reports orphaned or missing cache segments.

nodes.name: install-global-git-hooks.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that installs the ECC Codex git hooks (pre-commit, pre-push) into the global git hooks directory for system-wide activation.

nodes.name: merge-codex-config.js
nodes.summary: Codex configuration merge utility that performs add-only TOML merging from the ECC .codex/config.toml baseline into a target config file, preserving existing user values.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Primary merge orchestrator that reads source and target TOML files, diffs missing keys and tables, and writes the merged result back to disk.

nodes.name: dashboard-web.js
nodes.summary: ECC capabilities web dashboard that serves an HTTP server on port 3456, rendering agents, skills, commands, rules, MCPs, and hooks as a searchable multi-language HTML UI.

nodes.name: renderHTML
nodes.summary: Generates the complete HTML page for the ECC capabilities dashboard including all CSS, JavaScript, data loading, and rendering logic as a self-contained string.

nodes.name: ecc-bot.mjs
nodes.summary: Dependency-free Discord bot for the ECC community that responds to slash commands (/ecc, /skill, /docs, /release) using the local ECC repo as a data source and native Node.js WebSockets.

nodes.name: respond
nodes.summary: Routes Discord interaction events to the appropriate command handler and sends the reply via the Discord REST API.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Establishes the Discord Gateway WebSocket connection, handles heartbeat/reconnect, and dispatches interaction events to the respond handler.
